hello, we have seen the daemonize() issue when trying to rebase on master. We are working on building a new running stack by stacking all the patches reviewed in gerrit. After we will rebase on master and push new version of patches (for the ones which need).

Patch Set 7:

JC, when you update (not rebase but update) this patch I have a suggestion about hsm_get_md_attr(). I have already objected to the multiplexing behavior of this function, but I guess it will not be overhauled. So in the interest of error detection, if we are putting obj then please set it to NULL before returning it. Then on successful return IS_ERR() will return false and callers cannot mistakenly modify some other object. And please add a conspicuous comment to this effect.

Patch Set 7: (29 inline comments)

There are too much code here in this patch so I can't review them all once.

Patch Set 10:

The failure was due to OST full.

failure on sanity:101d was obviously
failure on sanity:102b was because it couldn't allocate the object from that full OST so the stripe count became 6.

I'll retrigger the test.

I'll fix this problem so that it can be tested over weekend.

Patch Set 10:

The issue comes from the new cache added after the review is initialized per MDT device and not per MDT module.
I will push a fix soon (with another change Jinshan requested)

parents.subject: LU-3633 test: Sanity test 101d fails due to full OST

commit.subject: LU-3343 mdt: HSM coordinator main thread
commit.message:
LU-3343 mdt: HSM coordinator main thread

This patch implements the HSM coordinator. It is a MDT
thread in charge of schedulling HSM requests to
agents.

Signed-off-by: JC Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I18fb2fad94b3972b9a09fd093e259f4ad50d810f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6912
Tested-by: Hudson
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
